sections.paragraph:
ἄτονος, ον (ἀτονέω ‘be relaxed, not stretched or in torsion’; Theophr., HP 3, 18, 11; Plut.; Epict. 3, 16, 7; Vett. Val. 233, 12 and15; 337, 19; PStras 95, 11; PLond VI, 1929, 14; Job 5:16 Aq.; Ps 81:3 Sym.; Jos., Bell. 1, 203; 3, 114; Tat. 32, 2) slack, powerless of the devil’s threats ἄ. ὥσπερ νεκροῦ νεῦρα as powerless as the sinews of a corpse Hm 12, 6, 2 (ἄ. w. νεκρός as Epict., loc. cit.).—DELG s.v. τανυ-E. τείνω.

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PStras PStras = Griechische Papyrus der kaiserlichen Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek zu Strassburg—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

loc. loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 149
